page: Amazon Web Services i7i.4xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 2.4 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by a 320 MB L3 cache and advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX for tile and int8 operations. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 3750 GB NVMe SSD, positioning it for high-throughput data access. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 9 Gbps. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the i7i.4xlarge demonstrates strong memory write capabilities, with large block write bandwidth reaching 91.67 GB/sec, placing it in the top 25%. Geekbench single-core performance is in the top 10% with a score of 2308.0, and text processing also ranks in the top 10% at 2840.0. Single-threaded gzip compression is strong at 34.75 GB/sec. However, multi-core CPU performance and database operations fall within the average middle 50% range, with a Passmark CPU score of 29132.42 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 13730.0. Small model LLM text generation performs in the top 10% at 395.0 tokens/sec, while larger model inference remains average.

The hardware profile offers high resource density, combining fast DDR5 memory with substantial local NVMe storage. This makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring rapid local data retrieval and high memory bandwidth without the overhead of external storage. The server is suited for relational databases, NoSQL data stores, text processing pipelines, and lightweight LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.4xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz and a bundled 3750 GB NVMe SSD for high-speed local storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2308.0 and strong memory write bandwidth of 91.67 GB/sec. While multi-core CPU and database operations perform in the average tier, the instance's high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1:8 and fast local NVMe storage provide a balanced profile for data-intensive applications. This server is suited for database hosting, text processing, and memory-intensive workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.4xlarge is a storage-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3750 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core CPU and strong memory write performance.

tagline: A storage-optimized platform combining high-speed DDR5 memory and local NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive workloads.

128.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Bundled 3750 GB local NVMe SSD for high-throughput storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Strong memory write bandwidth of 91.67 GB/sec
Suited for database hosting, text processing, and memory-intensive applications
